description: 42 - 24-12-2024 - Email from Overseas Team FCDO, to British Embassy in Washington team FCDO, and others
text:
in Washington team FCDO, and others

From: UK HOM Postings{/ Personal

Sent: Tue 24/12/2024 10:29:52 AM (UTC)

To: JCS

Ce: James Roscoe Personal ; Robert
Tinline Personal : HRD Senior
Appointments Personal ; UK HOM
Postings Personal

Subject: HMA-designate Washington - draft Press Announcement

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Dear All,

Lord Mandelson is due to take up his posting as the next Ambassador to the United
States in January 2025 in succession to Dame Karen Pierce.
HRD Senior Appointments have informed me that Third Party

and Lord Mandelson's is in progress. As we may not hear anything further
until the New Year, there is a chance this will hold up the issue of Lord Mandelson’ s
contract.
Our normal announcing process is to prepare and clear a standard draft
announcement with the incumbent HoM, HoM-designate and geographical
department, ask when they would like the announcement to be made, and if there is a
need for wider clearance (through Special Advisers). We then clear the whole
announcement and the timing with the PUS's Office, and then send it to Newsdesk to
announce on the agreed date. We ask the HoM-designate to send us a suitable photo
as the announcement cannot be made without a photo.
If the draft announcement has to be cleared with No 10, this could delay things so |
thought it best to warn you. Karen Pierce's announcement wasn't in the standard
format and was done by Americas/Comms with heavy involvement from No 10
https:/Awww.gov.uk/government/news/karen-pierce-appointed-as-her-majestys-
ambassado! e-united-states-of-america so we expect Lord Mandelson's will be
similar especially given that his cv is non-standard.
Please let me know if you need anything further from me at this stage. As normal, the
announcement shouldn't be made until agrément has been granted (though this is a
moot point given the PM has already announced it).
Kind Regards,

JCS
JCS | Overseas Team - People Data Management, HR Services, Foreign,
Commonwealth & Development Office | Personal | Mobile: Persona!

HR Services are continually working to improve - you can help by telling us what you
think

142

OFFICIAL 
Lord Mandelson & Minister Vallance 
Date: 11 September 2024 
Attendees: Minister Vallance (PV), Lord Mandelson (PM) 
PM opening remarks 
� 
PM noted potential plans for an industrial strategy council and highlighted past 
experience with xWH initiatives of this sort after the 2008 crisis. PM added that 
he views the work on the S&T Framework as aligned with this approach to 
governance. 
� 
PM added that he hopes R&D spending won't come under pressure and isn't 
clear on whether or not this government will protect it in the same way as may 
have been done in the past. 
o
PV noted examples in the private sector that show maintaining R&D
spend is important even in challenging times.
� 
PM brieﬂy mentioned that the new CEO of UKRI, when appointed, should be 
working within direction/a policy framework from government. 
PV opening remarks 
� 
PV highlighted the importance of a joined up approach regarding procurement in 
relation to R&D spending, as this is important for company growth. 
DSIT work broadly. 
� 
PM noted the work of DSIT on being the digital centre of government and on AI. 
� 
PV mentioned that DSIT is to play a very important role in these areas among 
others. 
S&T Framework 
� 
PV noted that the S&T Framework is still important and PV hopes there will be 
xWH collaboration across the diﬀerent areas that S&T crosses over. PV added the 
importance of Prime Ministerial authority and a driver in the centre to ensure 
this, and brieﬂy referred to the Cabinet Committee on S&T.
parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
